provenanceJourney.event: Made for Francis I of France
Given by Charles IX to Archduke Ferdinand II of Tyrol
Enters the imperial collections, later the Kunsthistorisches Museum
Recovered buried in woodland; returned to the museum

redFlags.evidence: Stolen from the Kunsthistorisches Museum on 11 May 2003 via scaffolding on the facade; recovered in January 2006, buried in a lead box in woodland near Zwettl, Lower Austria. The thief was convicted.

repatriation.note: Continuous documented ownership since the 16th century and undisputed title. The 2003 theft is a closed criminal episode, not a provenance defect — included here precisely because a register hit is not always a title problem.

registryChecks.registry: INTERPOL Stolen Works of Art Database
registryChecks.method: Domain-scoped search of interpol.int. This searches the registry's public WEBSITE, not its register.
registryChecks.caveat: No evidence surfaced through the access available. This is NOT a statement that the object is absent from this register, and it is NOT a clean bill of health — objects looted at source were never inventoried and cannot appear in a stolen-property register at all.

registryChecks.method: No programmatic interface exists. Deep link emitted for a human to run the authoritative search.
registryChecks.caveat: This register could not be searched programmatically. Nothing about the object has been established either way — the official search below must be run by a human.

registryChecks.method: SPARQL over Q697208 (Cellini Salt Cellar) — significant-event (P793), collection (P195), origin (P495), discovery (P189). Chosen from 1 label-search candidate(s): Q697208 Cellini Salt Cellar. Entity resolution is shown so the choice can be audited rather than trusted.
